p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot; dense foliage cluster; consistent leaf count.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Remained stable throughout the 5-day observation period.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: Healthy; turgid leaves with no signs of chlorosis or wilting.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot; two primary leaves and smaller central growth.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: Gradual decline in leaf posture; initial upright stance transitioned to a slight droop by T-1.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Stressed; leaf margins show signs of dehydration-induced curling.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot; two leaves; white rabbit scale anchor present.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The apical leaf has shown progressive necrosis at the tip, moving from 2mm to 5mm of browning.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Declining; tip necrosis suggests potential over-watering or nutrient imbalance.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Black pot; small sprout near rim.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Showed signs of desiccation; the leaf has curled significantly since the earliest image.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_inference: Critical; high probability of root failure or insufficient moisture uptake.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil in p2/p4 black pot appears increasingly dry and compacted.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: No weeds or moss detected; soil surface remains clear.
biome_observations.anomalies: Debris (likely leaf fragments) noted on the desk surface near the black pots.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: The transition from Earliest to Current shows a clear trend of moisture loss in the black pots (p2, p3, p4), while the yellow pot (p1) remains unaffected.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The ecosystem is experiencing a localized drought effect in the black pots. The Pothos (p3) and Silver Guest (p4) are the most vulnerable.
vision_report.anomalies: The white rabbit anchor has remained stationary, confirming no physical disturbance to the pots.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is bifurcated in health. The String of Nickels (p1) is thriving, while the plants in the black pots are showing signs of environmental stress, likely due to substrate moisture depletion. The Pothos (p3) is exhibiting progressive tip necrosis, and the Silver Guest (p4) is showing signs of severe dehydration.
